topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone screen, a stark intrusion in the otherwise quiet afternoon. My breath hitched. It was a name I hadn't thought about in over a decade, a name synonymous with sharp words and pointed stares. My fingers hovered over the screen, a strange mixture of dread and something else—a faint, almost imperceptible easing—running through my chest. I stared at the profile picture: a slightly distorted, overly confident grin that used to send shivers down my spine. Then, a single, hesitant tap. A message window opened. The initial text was short, almost apologetic, a clumsy attempt at reconnection. I let out a slow, shaky exhale, the tension that had been coiled tight in my shoulders finally beginning to unwind. I leaned back in my chair, the stiffness in my neck dissolving.

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my mug of tea. The screen glowed with an unfamiliar contact name. It was Mark. Mark, the architect of my middle school nightmares. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo against the silence of my apartment. For a long moment, I just stared at the 'Accept Request' button, my hand trembling slightly before finally committing. Once it was done, the immediate, suffocating pressure lifted. I pressed my palms flat against the cool wooden desk, feeling the frantic energy drain away, replaced by a vast, empty space. I stood up, stretching my arms above my head until my spine popped, a deep, satisfying release washing over me.

[story 3]

A strange warmth spread through my palms as I saw the familiar profile icon pop up on my feed. It was him. The memory of his sneering face was sharp, almost painful, but this time, it wasn't a threat; it was just a digital ghost. I clicked through the prompt, reading the opening line. It was awkward, laced with years of unspoken history. A small, involuntary smile tugged at the corner of my mouth, and I found myself leaning forward, genuinely curious. The knot that had been tightening in my stomach for months finally loosened its grip. I took a deep, steadying breath, the air feeling cleaner in my lungs, and started typing a response, the words flowing out with an unforced ease.
